mappings.rationale: Provides an overview of the branches of mathematics including algebra, analysis, geometry, and combinatorics, which are the core topics of this section.
Section B covers algebra comprehensively from arithmetic through algebraic structures including groups, rings, fields, and categories.
Section B.1 covers arithmetic including natural numbers, integers, binary operations, number systems, and logarithms.
Section C covers Euclidean, non-Euclidean, projective, analytic, differential, and algebraic geometry in comprehensive detail.
Section C.4.b specifically covers trigonometry as part of analytic and trigonometric geometry.
Section F covers topology in detail including general topology, algebraic topology, topological spaces, and manifold theory.
Section E.2 covers number theory including elementary, algebraic, analytic, geometric, and probabilistic number theory.
Section E.1 covers combinatorics and combinatorial geometry, including methods, results, and unsolved problems.
Section D.6 covers the theory of probability including probability on finite and infinite dimensional spaces.
Statistics is grounded in the theory of probability covered in section D.6 and is closely related to the analysis topics in section D.
Set theory (section A) deals fundamentally with infinite sets including Cantorian set theory and the axioms of Zermelo-Fraenkel.
Section A.3.b discusses Gödel's theorem as a limitation of axiomatic set theory, demonstrating the failure to prove consistency.
Section B.4.b covers group theory, which is the mathematical formalization of symmetry, and section C.1.c covers transformation geometry.
Fractals relate to topology (section F), the concept of dimension, and special curves and geometric objects covered in sections C and D.
Section D covers analysis in comprehensive detail including real analysis, complex analysis, differential equations, functional analysis, and Fourier analysis.
Section D.3.d covers dynamical systems on manifolds, and chaos theory is a key branch of the study of nonlinear differential equations in section D.3.
Set theory (section A) is deeply connected to logic, and sections A.3.a-b discuss axiomatic foundations and Gödel's theorem.
Many branches covered here (differential equations, Fourier analysis, vector/tensor analysis) have extensive applications, bridging pure and applied mathematics.
Mathematical finance draws on probability theory (D.6), stochastic processes, and analysis (D), representing a major application of these branches.
